task_name: Apple Health Multi-Source Health Data Import

continuity.arc_goal: Consolidate Withings, Apple Health, Evernote, and Oura tag data into health.db to enable cross-pillar correlation queries via iMessage (HRV vs workout intensity, BP vs sleep, body fat% vs BP trend, sauna tags vs recovery metrics). Multi-session data source integration on top of working read layer (health_query.py, cost_summary.py, health-query SKILL.md) with BP pillar complete, weekly email, and spend tracking.

progress_update.summary: We scoped and planned a multi-source health data import system, then executed the first piece: Withings API integration. Scoping session defined 4 data sources (Withings API, Apple Health XML, Evernote ENEX, Oura tags) and 3 delivery sessions (Option A: Withings → Apple Health → Evernote+Oura). Full plan and task breakdown generated in docs/tasks/main/specs/. Then built withings-auth.py (one-time OAuth2 token exchange) and withings-sync.py (daily body composition cron mirroring oura-sync.py exactly), hit and fixed an SSL cert issue on macOS (switched from urllib to requests), and successfully ran --historical import: 154 unique weigh-in dates from May 2025–April 2026 now in body_metrics table.
progress_update.goal: Consolidate Withings, Apple Health, Evernote, and Oura tag data into health.db to enable cross-pillar correlation queries via iMessage (HRV vs workout intensity, BP vs sleep, body fat% vs BP trend, sauna tags vs recovery metrics).
accomplished: Scoped feature across 4 data sources; created docs/tasks/main/concepts/scope_apple_health_multi_source.md
Full research (4 parallel agents): Withings API OAuth quirks, Apple Health XML identifiers, Evernote ENEX format, codebase patterns — all documented
Comprehensive plan at docs/tasks/main/specs/plan.md (3-session Option A delivery)
32 sub-task breakdown at docs/tasks/main/specs/tasks.md (20 parent tasks, phases A/B/C)
Added PRAGMA user_version migration framework to health_db.py; added body_metrics table (schema version 2)
Built scripts/withings-auth.py — one-time OAuth2 token exchange (fixed SSL cert issue using requests instead of urllib)
Built scripts/withings-sync.py — daily cron mirroring oura-sync.py exactly; 154 rows imported successfully, May 2025–April 2026
progress_update.now: Session A partially complete. Withings sync is live with real data. Remaining Session A tasks: body-metrics subcommand in health_query.py (task 1.5), Rule 6b extension in AGENTS.md (task 1.6), unit tests (task 1.7). Ready for /spectre:execute on these remaining tasks.
next_steps: Run /spectre:execute to complete remaining Session A tasks: body-metrics query subcommand, Rule 6b wiring, tests
Session B: Apple Health XML import (all categories: BP, weight, body fat, steps, daylight, workouts)
Session C: Evernote ENEX parser + Oura tags + full SKILL.md/AGENTS.md wiring
constraints: Withings OAuth tokens in agents/sample-agent/.env — access token expires ~3h, withings-sync.py refreshes proactively
body_metrics table now at schema version 2 (PRAGMA user_version = 2)
Apple Health XML export drop folder: ~/Downloads/apple_health_export/export.xml
Evernote: manual ENEX export only (API closed); filter by title regex 'Week \d+ Training Plan'; Jan 2025 forward
All body measurements in lbs (kg converted on import); Withings API source takes priority over apple_health in body_metrics
AGENTS.md already ~48K chars (~17% truncated) — new Rule 6b blocks must be concise (≤4 lines each)
withings-auth.py uses requests (not urllib) to avoid macOS SSL cert issue
health_query.py runs inside agent container — paths must be container-relative
Python 3 only, no jq, no new pip deps beyond stdlib + sqlite3
health.db at workspace/health/health.db — imported via sys.path.insert
Perplexity already configured in openclaw.json, now enabled: true
BlueBubbles is sole active channel — Telegram disabled (config preserved)
decisions: Option A (source-by-source delivery): Session A=Withings, Session B=Apple Health XML all categories, Session C=Evernote+Oura tags
Withings developer app registered in integration phase (10-user limit) — fine for personal use
Fixed drop folder for Apple Health XML with --file override CLI arg
Evernote filter: tagged export + title regex 'Week \d+ Training Plan'
body_metrics table schema: date, metric_type (weight/body_fat), value_lbs, source (withings/apple_health/manual), imported_at, dedup_key — safe for concurrent multi-source writes
Two separate AGENTS.md rules for health data: Rule 6b (query enforcement) and Rule 6c (entry enforcement) — different failure modes require different forbidden phrases
bp_sessions.py is single source of truth for session grouping — future pillars import from it
All planning docs now live in ~/Code Projects/{Project Name}/ — not inside ironclaw repo docs/tasks/
risks: Withings tokens must be refreshed before expiry — withings-sync.py handles this but first daily cron run must succeed
Apple Health XML can be 200MB-2GB — streaming iterparse mandatory
AGENTS.md growing large (~48K chars system prompt, ~17% truncated) — Rule 6b entries must stay concise as more pillars added

context.session_arc: 9-session sprint on health-db multi-pillar system (Apr 26–29). S1 scoped health-db. S2 implemented phases 1-3 (SQLite, blood labs, Oura). S3 wrote 4 test files. S4 clean/test/evaluate, fixed 3 architecture issues, pushed, disabled Telegram. S5 scoped health_query.py (3 subcommands, 6 intents), shipped raw_transcript storage. S6 executed health_query.py: built all 3 subcommands, cost_summary.py, health-query SKILL.md, enabled Perplexity, 88 tests passing, pushed 5 commits. S7 debugged live routing: fixed AGENTS.md Rule 6b enforcement, per-intent guardrails in SKILL.md, skill config sync issue, and intent triggers. S8 built BP pillar end-to-end (import, query, entry, tests, SKILL.md Intents 7+8, AGENTS.md Rules 6b+6c), fixed 3 arch review issues, 72 tests passing, 14 commits pushed. Agent live and responding correctly. S9 scoped multi-source data import (Withings, Apple Health XML, Evernote ENEX, Oura tags) and completed Withings integration: OAuth2 token setup, daily cron with historical import (154 rows). Next: Apple Health XML import (Session B), Evernote+Oura tags (Session C).
